title: Fix editor diagnostics cross-file contamination and file-switch race conditions
analysis:
Four bugs around editor file switching need fixing in the SolidJS/CodeMirror6 editor component:

(E-05) Diagnostics cross-file contamination: The `createDiagnosticsListener` callback in Editor.tsx (lines 144-157) applies incoming diagnostics to the editor without checking whether `event.uri` matches `currentUri`. Diagnostics from file A get applied to file B when you switch files.

(RC-04) Debounced didChange after file switch: The `lspDebounceTimer` (lines 107-113) and `debounceTimer` (lines 99-104) are NOT cleared when the file switch effect runs (lines 168-195). A pending timer from editing file A could fire after switching to file B. While the captured `path` variable is correct (captured at edit time), the shared `lspVersion` counter creates conflicts, and sending didChange for a URI that was just didClose'd confuses the LSP server.

(E-04) File switch replaces doc content instead of swapping EditorState: Lines 183-186 use `view.dispatch({changes: ...})` to replace the entire document on file switch. This pollutes the undo history — undoing in file B would restore file A's content. Fix: maintain a `Map<string, EditorState>` of per-file states, save current state on switch, restore the target file's state via `view.setState()`.

(RC-02) LSP didClose/didOpen not serialized: Lines 191-194 fire `didClose(oldUri).then(() => didOpen(newUri))` but the promise chain is fire-and-forget. Rapid file switches (A->B->C) can cause overlapping didClose/didOpen calls that arrive out of order. Fix: chain each file switch's LSP operations off a `fileOpsPromise` variable to serialize them.

steps.description: Write failing test: E-05 — diagnostics from non-active URI should not be applied to editor. In Editor.test.tsx, add a test in a new 'Editor diagnostics URI filtering' describe block. Simulate a Tauri diagnostics event by capturing the listen handler from the mock, then invoke it with a payload whose `uri` does NOT match the active file's URI. Assert that the editor's diagnostics remain empty (no `cm-diagnostic` elements in DOM). Also add a positive test: diagnostics whose URI matches the active file ARE applied.

Fix E-05 — filter diagnostics by URI. In the `createDiagnosticsListener` callback in Editor.tsx (around line 144), add a guard: `if (event.uri !== currentUri) return;` before converting and applying diagnostics. This ensures only diagnostics for the currently active file are applied to the editor.

Write failing test: RC-04 — debounced didChange should not fire after file switch. In Editor.test.tsx, add a test that: (1) opens file1 in editor, (2) dispatches an edit to the CM6 view (triggering debounce timers), (3) immediately switches to file2 via store.setActiveFile (before 300ms elapses), (4) advances timers past 300ms with vi.advanceTimersByTime(300), (5) asserts that bridge.updateSource was NOT called for the stale edit (timer should have been cancelled by the file switch).

Fix RC-04 — clear debounce timers on file switch. In the `createEffect` for active file changes in Editor.tsx (around line 168), add `clearTimeout(debounceTimer); clearTimeout(lspDebounceTimer);` right after the early return check and before performing the document swap. This cancels any pending debounced operations from the previous file.

Write failing test: E-04 — file switch should preserve per-file undo history (EditorState swap). In Editor.test.tsx, add a test that: (1) opens file1, (2) makes an edit to file1 (insert '// edit\n' at position 0), (3) confirms edit is in the doc, (4) switches to file2, (5) confirms file2 content is shown, (6) switches back to file1, (7) confirms file1 content includes the edit, (8) dispatches undo command, (9) asserts file1 content reverts to original — proving undo history is per-file. A second test verifies that undoing in file2 after switching does NOT produce file1's content.

Fix E-04 — swap per-file EditorState instead of replacing doc content. Add a `Map<string, EditorState>` (fileStates) to Editor.tsx. Extract the CM6 extensions array into a `const extensions = [...]` variable before `EditorState.create()`. In the file switch effect: (1) save `view.state` into `fileStates.set(previousActiveFile, view.state)`, (2) check if `fileStates.has(activeFile)` — if yes, `view.setState(fileStates.get(activeFile)!)`, otherwise create a fresh `EditorState.create({doc: newContent, extensions})` and use `view.setState()`. Remove the old `view.dispatch({changes: ...})` approach and the `isSwitching` flag (no longer needed since setState doesn't trigger updateListener).

Write failing test: RC-02 — rapid file switches should serialize LSP didClose/didOpen. In Editor.test.tsx, add a test with 3 files (file1, file2, file3). Mock lspClient methods to track call order. Switch file1->file2 then immediately file2->file3 (two setActiveFile calls in sequence). Flush all promises. Assert the LSP call order is exactly: didClose(uri1), didOpen(uri2), didClose(uri2), didOpen(uri3) — proving serialization (each pair completes before the next begins).

Fix RC-02 — serialize LSP file operations with a promise chain. Add `let fileOpsPromise: Promise<void> = Promise.resolve()` to Editor.tsx. In the file switch effect, replace the fire-and-forget promise chain with: `fileOpsPromise = fileOpsPromise.then(() => lspClient.didClose(oldUri)).then(() => lspClient.didOpen(newUri, newContent, lspVersion)).catch(err => console.error('LSP file switch error:', err))`. This ensures each file switch's LSP calls complete before the next pair starts.

Write integration-level test: rapid file switch with diagnostics arriving mid-switch. In Editor.test.tsx, combine all four fixes: (1) open files A and B, (2) edit file A, (3) switch A->B, (4) fire a diagnostics event for file A's URI, (5) advance timers past debounce period, (6) verify: diagnostics NOT applied to B's editor, debounced updateSource NOT called, LSP calls correctly ordered. This serves as a regression test for the combined behavior.

Final cleanup: ensure onCleanup handler also clears the fileStates map. Remove the `isSwitching` flag and guard if it's no longer needed (setState doesn't trigger updateListener). Run the full gui test suite to confirm no regressions across all test files.

design_decisions.decision: Use a Map<string, EditorState> for per-file state preservation
design_decisions.rationale: CodeMirror 6's EditorState is immutable and serializable. Storing a snapshot per file path allows perfect restoration of undo history, cursor position, and scroll position when switching back. This is the standard CM6 pattern for multi-tab editors.

design_decisions.decision: Use view.setState() instead of view.dispatch({changes}) for file switching
design_decisions.rationale: view.dispatch() with content replacement creates a transaction in the undo history — undoing would restore the previous file's content. view.setState() replaces the entire state cleanly without polluting history. It also doesn't trigger the updateListener, eliminating the need for the `isSwitching` guard flag.

design_decisions.decision: Serialize LSP file ops via promise chaining rather than an async queue
design_decisions.rationale: A simple promise chain (`fileOpsPromise = fileOpsPromise.then(...)`) is the minimal solution that guarantees ordering without introducing a queue abstraction. Each file switch chains off the previous one. Errors are caught per-chain-link so one failure doesn't block subsequent switches.

design_decisions.decision: Clear debounce timers at the start of file switch effect rather than in the timer callbacks
design_decisions.rationale: Clearing timers eagerly on file switch is simpler and more robust than adding guards inside the timer callbacks. It ensures no stale work runs after the context has changed.

design_decisions.decision: Filter diagnostics by URI in the Editor callback, not in diagnostics.ts
design_decisions.rationale: The diagnostics.ts module is a generic event bridge — it shouldn't know about the active file concept. The URI filtering belongs in the Editor component which has the `currentUri` context. This keeps diagnostics.ts reusable.

design_decisions.decision: Extract CM6 extensions into a shared variable for reuse in fresh EditorState creation
design_decisions.rationale: When a file is opened for the first time (no saved EditorState in the map), we need to create a fresh EditorState with the same extensions. Extracting the extensions array avoids duplication between onMount and the file switch effect.

reuse.how: Reuse for all new Editor tests to get the CM6 view instance from the rendered container

reuse.what: Existing setupStore() helper and file fixtures (file1, file2)
reuse.how: Extend with a file3 fixture and reuse setupStore for multi-file test scenarios

reuse.what: Existing Tauri API mocks (vi.mock for @tauri-apps/api/core and event)
reuse.how: Reuse the same mock patterns; extend the listen mock to simulate diagnostics events with URI fields

reuse.what: DiagnosticsEvent type with uri field already defined
reuse.how: The event payload already includes `uri: string` — we just need to check it in the Editor callback
Already available in scope for URI comparison in diagnostics filtering
Reuse the same extensions for creating fresh per-file EditorState instances in the fileStates map
